title: International Eva store
wikitext:
[]: International Eva-store?             Quote Post by All_is_Right » Thu Apr 04, 2013 8:17 pm I don't know how many of you guys are interested in the idea...but how would you like an official Eva-store that ships internationally? Is it worth making? http://www.evastore.jp/pc/ (like this one, but not only shipping in Japan) If you are interested please like this page: https://www.facebook.com/pages/Evangelion-Fan/500479889999813 My boss will be more convinced to go through with it if there is international fan support.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Apr 04, 2013 8:38 pm Do you work for Khara/Gainax or some other official licensor? Or would it be an unofficial international Eva store? It sounds VERY interesting either way.

[]: reply             Quote Post by All_is_Right » Thu Apr 04, 2013 9:38 pm We would set up a meeting with them. I work for a Japanese marketing company that is unrelated, but my boss knows Hideaki Anno, apparently (I didn't even know this until he started talking about having some connection to Evangelion) I don't do anything related to Evangelion, I'm just the only foreign employee around...

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Thu Apr 04, 2013 10:59 pm This would be nice but VERY hard, Japanese are either clueless or ignorant to their foreign fans. i had some contacts onbandai, and can you believe it took them freaking 4 years to actually listen to a sugestion i made via a distributor to them ? Only know bandai is starting to see their foreign costumers as you know, costumers.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Apr 05, 2013 3:38 am Eh, what the heck. I'll throw a like your way if it even has the potential of making this happen. :) And if it does happen . . .

[]:             Quote Post by lastcody » Fri Apr 05, 2013 9:22 am Wait did Evageeks just find a direct link to Anno himself...duuuude. & HELL YEAH I want a international EVA store. If this ain't no foolin' then I'll do anything to get it over into the west.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Fri Apr 05, 2013 9:59 am another facebook content aggregator... yay...

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Fri Apr 05, 2013 11:02 am If this is real, I'm confused and excited and horny as fuck.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Apr 05, 2013 9:42 pm ^ Sigged. What threshold do you need to get taken seriously? Couple hundred? Thousands? Million??? I would say also link eva-t.jp in so the world can wear Eva with pride (someone I know in Japan is doing an order for me ATM) but the store first, then the rest!

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Sat Apr 06, 2013 12:59 am If you're serious about this I suggest trying Kickstarter and see if you can raise interest (and funds) for a web-based storefront. I'd love to see a webstore that's entirely dedicated to EVA. I doubt GAINAX & Khara will see the value of a global store, as to them 95% of the EVA market is in Japan*. That's not to say you couldn't just buy as much as you can wholesale from Japanese and other sources and then just sell it all online. *It's not, of course, but it's difficult convincing the Japanese of that fact.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Sat Apr 06, 2013 5:48 am GUYS, GUYS the page OP linked is just a content aggregator(which OP wants to probably make money off through advertising in the future), it doesn't even mention international eva store ONCE . It takes Sadamoto art, slaps the group name on it and is done. Even we had more of a chance to convince Khara to do anything with the users of evageeks than with a group that coasted at 23 likes OP's not coming back either

[]:             Quote Post by All_is_Right » Mon Apr 08, 2013 8:54 pm Hi, OP here... I actually run multiple fb pages all with the main goal of seeing what fb users in different regions respond to....I do work for a marketing company (which I think I was pretty upfront about), so it's a bit our goal.... SE Asia is pretty popular right now and I have been doing more with that (the anime fans there tend to be younger and in large numbers) to be honest and my boss is a bit capricious...So I'm actually pretty unsure about the viability of this happening.... I feel really bad for getting evageeks hopes up (I actually had hopes up as well, sigh) COMPLETELY UNRELATED but...if you are in Japan and are interested in possibly meeting I might be at one of these: http://eva-con.info/ because I am super tempted.....

[]:             Quote Post by Tolwyn » Fri Apr 26, 2013 10:31 am The official EVA shop did supply the international market till a couple of years ago, then they stoped for the simple reason that most of the stuff the are selling are licensed only for Japan. So they are simply not allowed to sell to international customers. Which is very unfortunately as i can t visit their store in Tokyo that often :(

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jan 01, 2015 5:28 pm To rebump this old topic: http://www.evastore.jp/pc/info-oversea.html https://otakumode.com/shop/series-characters/evangelion I've had my eye on a few little things from Otaku Mode, I had no idea until today they were an official Eva Store seller!

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jan 21, 2015 1:00 pm Hmmm, interesting. The fact that it's an English website suggests I might be able to make inquiries, such as whether I can get Misato's shoulder holster in a leftie version or whether I can get a version of the Kamikaze jacket that would actually fit me. I'm not even fat these days, but even so Japan doesn't seem interested in making apparel for six-foot-tall people!

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Jan 21, 2015 7:35 pm I didn't check Otakumode's markup. How high is it? There is always the deputy or proxy service route. Seriously, nothing beat being able to pay the ¥ prices in Japan and knowing how much of a savings that was. I was getting a better exchange rate too with my card since the ¥ got weaker as the trip went on and I only changed $ once. If you like UFO catcher figures be very careful with the pricing on those - there is no cover price so retailers will gouge you easily. Yet there are places in Akiba where you can buy such figs for maybe 2x of what it cost someone to win them. Saw some Petit Eva mini figs for 200¥ and those could easily sell for $5+.

[]:             Quote Post by deeRez » Fri Jan 23, 2015 1:20 am Eva-store has a shop on Yahoo, which shows up on buyee.jp (click Shopping tab instead of Auction tab). That's how I get my official eva-store stuff. http://buyee.jp/item/yahoo/shopping/evastore_A7023801 (click English in the top right)

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Jan 23, 2015 3:30 pm I got pointed to the shot glasses. This is the main link: http://store.shopping.yahoo.co.jp/evastore/a7023801.html
summary: []: International Eva-store?             Quote Post by All_is_Right » Thu Apr 04, 2013 8:17 pm I don't know how many of you guys are interested in the idea...but how would you like an official Eva-store that ships internationally? Is it worth making? http://www.evastore.jp/pc/ (like this one, but not only shipping in Japan) If you are interested please like this page: https://www.facebook.com/pages/Evangelion-Fan/500479889999813
